learning_outcomes.description: Recognise design manufacturing principles, materials, and technologies in the design context, and their impact on broader systems.
Communicate product design ideas using technical drawing conventions.
Experiment with and apply introductory subtractive and additive prototyping techniques based on design briefs
Use CAD models in digital fabrication processes to support prototyping and communicate design concepts
Justify use of prototyping methods to address specific design requirements across various contexts.
Communicate prototyping outcomes and design concepts effectively through written, visual, and verbal forms suited to peer audiences.

assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale:

In this design process assignment, you will document the tasks completed during the practical using a variety of mixed multimedia formats. Specific outputs will be included in the studio slides available on Blackboard. Upload your video, scans, and photos to Blackboard as a new journal entry by the end of the practical each week. Your design process will be reviewed for feedback purposes and marked during class the following week.

Tasks may include one or more of the following:

  • Photos of Sketching
  • Photos of prototyping
  • 5-10 second video. Quick overview showing: CAD work; Sketches; Prototyping
  • Quick Animation
  • Brief Reflection

In-person assessment prohibits use of AI or MT: This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The School of Architecture, Design and Planning uses Blackboard and Turnitin for assessment submission. Turnitin is accessed through the course Blackboard site. Turnitin also checks for plagiarism or instances where the original work of others is not appropriately acknowledged. Uploaded files must contain readable text and not be rasterised. Uploaded files must contain readable text and not be rasterised. Students are advised to commence assignment uploads with sufficient amount of time (consider possible technical problems with computers, internet speed, etc). After successfully submitting an assignment through Turnitin, a ‘Submission Complete!’ screen will be displayed. It is the student’s responsibility to check assignment preview and confirm successful submission. If the ‘Submission Complete!’ screen isn’t displayed, the student should regard the submission as unsuccessful. Students should download a copy of the digital receipt as proof they have submitted the assignment. Students who are experiencing upload issues must advise the Course Coordinator immediately by email and should include screenshots and a copy of the assessment for submission. To meet professional accreditation, public engagement and quality assurance obligations, digital copies of all course assessment items must be submitted in addition to any hard copy submission requirements specified in individual Course / Studio Outlines. Any physical models should be photographed and a minimum of two photographs describing the complete and full model must be included in the digital submission.

assessments.due_date:
Part 1 26/08/2026 5:00 pm

Part 2 9/09/2026 5:00 pm

Part 3 7/10/2026 5:00 pm

Digital Submission: Via Blackboard
assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale:

Model all three (3) components of your trophy designed for the Design Award in Autodesk Fusion 360 that utilise the capabilities of each advanced prototyping technology.

This is a 3-part submission that must include:

Part 1 - 3D Printed Main Body (10%)

  • You will use Autodesk Fusion to model the main form of your trophy that reflects your chosen design language.
  • The form must be modelled within the constraints of and optimised for FDM 3D printing.
  • Files must be ready for processing in .STL format – Renamed using the following naming convention: lastname_firstname_DSGN1400_AT23DP_part#of#
  • Submit all native CAD file(s) and .STL file(s) in Blackboard.

Part 2 – CNC-Machined Base (10%)

Using Autodesk Fusion, model the base of your trophy so that it expresses your chosen design language and can be CNC-machined from a solid block

Two distinct outputs, each demonstrating a different skill:

  1. Manufacturability (CAM).
  2. Production geometry (STEP). Export the final base model as a STEP file (.stp), the neutral 3D format the workshop uses to drive machining.

You will submit the following files to Blackboard:

  • Native Fusion file (.f3d) containing your CAM setup
  • STEP file (.stp) of the final base
  • A short screen-capture of the simulated toolpath (roughing + finishing)
  • File naming: lastname_firstname_DSGN1400_AT23DP_part#of#

Part 3 – Laser Cut Packaging (10%)

  • You will use Autodesk Fusion’s Sheet Metal tools to design packaging that reflects your chosen design language.
  • You will need to create a dieline of your design to be laser cut.
  • Files must be ready for processing in .DXF format – Renamed using the following naming convention: lastname_firstname_DSGN1400_AT23DP_part#of#.
  • Submit all native CAD file(s) and .DXF file(s) in Blackboard.

Complex / authentic assessment using AI and/or MT to support learning:  Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.

assessments.assessment_title: Design Trophy: Final Design and Artefact
assessments.due_date:
Digital Submission 28/10/2026 5:00 pm

Presentation and Exhibition in class 29/10/2026
assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale:

Tangible artefact and presentation due in class. In this project, you are invited to design and prototype a trophy for the Design Award. You will submit your final physical trophy design with the following outputs:

1. Behance Project Page (Design Documentation)

Create a Behance profile and upload one project that presents your trophy design. Include your final renders, your process work, and both animations.

Your project page must include:

  • A clear project title and a short introduction (2–3 sentences) explaining what the trophy is for and your design idea.
  • Final renders in high resolution: at least one hero image and 2–3 additional views, all with clean and consistent backgrounds.
  • Process work showing how your design developed (sketches, iterations, key decisions).
  • A short animation showing your trophy rotating 360 degrees (export from Fusion or similar software).

2. Process Video (Design Process and Development)

Create a short video explaining how your design developed from initial idea to the design outcome. Video should include:

  • Early sketches and initial ideas
  • Key design decisions and why you made them
  • Iterations and how your design changed over time
  • Your final design outcome

2. Physical Trophy (In-Class Presentation)

Bring your completed trophy to class for display and presentation for a 5-minutes presentation.

In-person assessment prohibits use of AI or MT: This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
